issue_title: Repeat mode UI becomes out of sync when switching videos from mini player

reasoning_trace.finding: Repeat state is handled in Player.getRepeatMode(), Player.cycleNextRepeatMode(), Player.onRepeatModeChanged(), VideoPlayerUi.onRepeatModeChanged(), NotificationPlayerUi.onRepeatModeChanged(), MediaSessionPlayerUi.onRepeatModeChanged(), and PlayQueueActivity.onPlayModeChanged.
The visible repeat button is @+id/repeatButton, initially using @drawable/exo_controls_repeat_off at line 621.
PlayerService is the media playback service and MainActivity/RouterActivity are app entry points; repeat UI symptoms are therefore in the player service/UI path.
VideoPlayerUi registers repeatButton clicks to onRepeatClicked(), which delegates to player.cycleNextRepeatMode(). Its onRepeatModeChanged(int) sets the repeatButton drawable for ALL, ONE, or OFF.
Player.cycleNextRepeatMode() changes simpleExoPlayer repeat mode, and ExoPlayer's onRepeatModeChanged callback forwards the new mode to all PlayerUi instances.
PlayerService creates or reuses Player, then Player.handleIntent() processes player intents and initializes the UI and playback queue.
NavigationHelper.playOnPopupPlayer(), playOnBackgroundPlayer(), and openMainPlayer paths start PlayerService with PLAYER_TYPE and a serialized PlayQueue, matching the report's video-switching flow.

When opening another video while a player exists, openMainPlayer() builds a new player intent; hideMainPlayerOnLoadingNewStream() may stop the current player for immediate reuse while keeping the UI binding reusable.
PlayerUiList.addAndPrepare() calls initPlayer() and initPlayback() when a UI is added to an already initialized player; initPlayback is the hook intended to sync UI state to current player state.

Player.initUIsForCurrentPlayerType() reuses an existing VideoPlayerUi binding when switching between main/popup video UIs. Player.initPlayback() destroys the current ExoPlayer, constructs a new one, initializes a new queue, then calls UIs.call(PlayerUi::initPlayback).
Player.initPlayer() creates a new ExoPlayer instance. A new ExoPlayer starts with default repeat mode OFF, but no explicit repeat UI refresh occurs here.
VideoPlayerUi.initPlayback() explicitly syncs the shuffle button using player.getExoPlayer().getShuffleModeEnabled(), but does not similarly sync repeatButton from player.getRepeatMode().

If the old reused repeatButton drawable showed ONE or ALL, a new ExoPlayer defaults to OFF. Since OFF is the default rather than a mode transition, ExoPlayer may not fire onRepeatModeChanged(OFF), so the UI remains stale.

The click logic correctly cycles OFF -> ONE -> ALL -> OFF and calls simpleExoPlayer.setRepeatMode(); it explains later inconsistent clicks only after the UI has already become stale, not the initial desync.
The drawable update logic correctly maps ALL, ONE, and OFF to the appropriate icons. The problem is that this method is not invoked during playback reinitialization.
These components update notification/media-session state on repeat callbacks, but the reported stale control is the in-player repeatButton owned by VideoPlayerUi.
PlayerHolder forwards playback updates with repeatMode to the fragment listener, but this affects overlay/listener state and does not update VideoPlayerUi.repeatButton.
The listener contract includes repeatMode in onPlaybackUpdate(), but VideoPlayerUi repeat drawable is not driven by this listener path.

reasoning_trace.target: Fault statement adjacent to missing repeat sync
reasoning_trace.finding: The precise fault location is VideoPlayerUi.initPlayback(), at the existing shuffle synchronization statement. It demonstrates the intended init-time state refresh but omits the analogous repeat refresh.

fault_candidates.reasoning: This initialization method refreshes shuffle UI state after playback is reinitialized, but it does not refresh repeat UI state. When switching videos from the mini player, Player.initPlayback() creates a new ExoPlayer whose repeat mode is OFF while the reused repeatButton drawable may still show ONE or ALL from the previous player. Because the repeat mode did not transition from a non-OFF value inside the new ExoPlayer, VideoPlayerUi.onRepeatModeChanged(OFF) is not guaranteed to run, leaving the button stale.

final_answer.causal_chain: User sets repeat ONE or ALL, so VideoPlayerUi.onRepeatModeChanged() changes repeatButton to the matching icon. The player is minimized and another video is opened. VideoDetailFragment/openMainPlayer sends a new PlayerService intent. Player.handleIntent() reaches Player.initPlayback(), which destroys the old ExoPlayer and creates a new ExoPlayer with default repeat mode OFF. The existing/reused VideoPlayerUi receives initPlayback(). That method refreshes shuffle state at line 402 but omits repeat state refresh. Since the new ExoPlayer is already OFF by default, no repeat-mode change callback is necessarily emitted. The repeatButton therefore keeps the old ONE/ALL drawable while player.getRepeatMode() is actually OFF, producing the reported desynchronization and inconsistent first press.
final_answer.fix_hint: In VideoPlayerUi.initPlayback(), also synchronize the repeat button from the current player state, e.g. call onRepeatModeChanged(player.getRepeatMode()) near the existing shuffle-button refresh.
